COUNSEL INVOLVED IN THIS CASE ARE EXPERIENCED ASBESTOS LITIGATORS. ON BOTH SIDES OF THE CASE, COUNSEL REGULARLY ARGUE FOR STRICT COMPLIANCE WITH THE MANDATES OF CCP SECTION 437C AND MOTIONS OFTEN TURN ON LITERAL COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS OF CCP SECTION 437C. THIS CASE IS AN EXAMPLE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S FAILURE TO SEEK A TIMELY CONTINUANCE IS UNFORTUNATE FOR THE CLIENTS. BUT IF THIS EXCUSE WERE ACCEPTED, IT WOULD UNDERMINE THE CONSTRAINTS OF CCP SECTION 437C. THE COURT ALSO FINDS THE HOLDING IN ENGLISH V. IKON BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC. (2001) 94 CAL. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Its Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Mar-26-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WARREN PUMPS, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Its Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues.

(403/PM) CLERK: FRANCES YOKOTA REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO #8600
Present in court were: Ingrid Evans for Plaintiff and Jeff Thayer for Defendant. The motion for reconsideration is ordered denied. In moving for summary judgment, defendant did not raise the distinction between exposure to original equipment and replacement equipment supplied by a third party. If defendant wishes to raise that issue, defendant needs to file a motion for summary judgment, not a motion for reconsideration. Order denying Defendant's motion for reconsideration signed in court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Ccp Section 473 To Vacate Order Granting Defendant Crane Co.S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Cook of Waters Kraus (310.414.8146) for plaintiffs and moving parties
Michele Barnes of K & L Gates (249.1011) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS CORNELL JANES, SUSAN JANES, SUSAN JANES AND PEGGY DEAN'S MOTION UNDER CCP SECTION 473 TO VACATE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CRANE CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Counsel involved in this case are experienced asbestos litigators. On both sides of the case, counsel regularly argue for strict compliance with the mandates of CCP section 437c and motions often turn on literal compliance with the procedural requirements of CCP section 437c. This case is an example. Plaintiff's counsel's failure to seek a timely continuance is unfortunate for the clients. But if this excuse were accepted, it would undermine the constraints of CCP section 437c. The Court also finds the holding in English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 138 persuasive. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO APRIL 16, 2008 PER EX PARTE STIPULATION AND ORDER ON MARCH 28, 2008. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR 11332

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WARREN PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL GURIEN, ESQUIRE (WATERSKRAUS, 310.414.8146) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JAMES P. CUNNINGHAM, ESQUIRE (CARROLL BURDICK & MCDONOUGH, 415.989.5900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING: denied. Defendant shifted the burden of proof by relying on plaintiff's discovery responses which lack sufficient detail to establish that decedent was exposed to defendant's product containing asbestos. In opposition, plaintiff has presented evidence of exposure to defendant's pumps and that asbestos was present. Defendant claims that plaintiff failed to show that the asbestos was original equipment. However in moving for summary judgment, defendant did not raise that as an issue and plaintiff was required to do no more than present evidence of exposure to defendant's product that contained exposure. Defendant has failed to establish that its actions were at the direction of a federal officer or that the pumps at issue were manufactured at the specific direction of the government or that the government limited or precluded warnings.
The motion for summary adjudication of the strict liability claim, the false representation claim, the intentional failure to warn are all based on the lack of exposure and plaintiff has demonstrated that there is a triable issue of fact on the issue of exposure. The motion for summary adjudication as to punitive damages is granted. Plaintiff's evidence consists solely of facts related to exposure and that is insufficient to provide a basis for punitive damages.
Evidentiary issues will be addressed at the hearing. COURT'S RULING ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS SERVED ON RESPECTIVE COUNSEL IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR 11332
